prompt:
Goal: Create a cute Heisei-era Japanese fashion magazine collage featuring Mikaze Kurage showing one full week of outfits, with a dreamy pastel blue, pink, and lavender “kawaii notebook diary” aesthetic.

Canvas: Vertical 2:3 poster, soft white paper background with faint pastel gradients, sparkles, doodled hearts, tiny jellyfish, stars, handwritten notes, and sticker-like borders. Use a glossy Y2K/Heisei retro fashion-magazine layout with rounded rectangular outfit cards.

Layout: Large title across the top in Japanese: 「御凪くらげの1週間コーデ」, with small furigana above the name and a small heart badge at upper left reading 「くらげの1Week おしゃれ日記」. Add a short intro paragraph in the upper right about introducing seven days of outfits. Arrange exactly 7 outfit cards: four cards across the upper/middle row for Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, and three cards across the lower left/middle for Friday, Saturday, Sunday. Place a large close-up portrait of the same character on the lower right, overlapping the Sunday area, posing with a peace sign and wearing a pale blue sheer cardigan. Add a bottom-left memo box and small speech-bubble doodles near the bottom.

Subject details: The character is a cute young woman with short platinum-white bob hair, soft pale skin, delicate makeup, and a faceless or softly blurred face. She has a jellyfish-themed, dreamy idol/fashion influencer vibe. Keep the body proportions photo-realistic but stylized like Japanese street fashion catalog photography.

Seven outfit cards, exactly counted and labeled:
1. Mon: pink lace girly coordinate, frilly pink top and mini skirt, white leg warmers, pink shoes, small handbag; label with pink heart “Mon” and Japanese caption about pink starting the week.
2. Tue: sporty school-inspired outfit, white cropped zip jacket with blue stripes, loose faded denim jeans with lace-up details, silver shoulder bag, white shoes; blue “Tue” label and sporty caption.
3. Wed: all-white layered lace outfit, white blouse, fluffy white mini skirt, white boots, cream handbag; purple “Wed” label and caption about fluffy white transparency.
4. Thu: black rock outfit, off-shoulder black top with corset details, gray plaid mini skirt, black lace stockings, chunky black boots, black shoulder bag; blue “Thu” label and caption about being cool in black.
5. Fri: elegant white lace long skirt coordinate, frilly white blouse, pale layered skirt, bonnet or head accessory, cream shoes and bag; pink “Fri” label and caption about going out dressed up.
6. Sat: casual shopping outfit, pale blue oversized cardigan over white top, short white skirt, socks and white shoes, silver bag; blue “Sat” label and caption about shopping with friends.
7. Sun: lavender sweet outfit, lavender graphic sweatshirt, white ruffle mini skirt, pale socks, light boots; purple “Sun” label and caption about relaxing in lavender.

Text content: Keep prominent Japanese text for authenticity, including the title 「御凪くらげの1週間コーデ」, the phrase 「今日のわたしがいちばんすき♡」 near the large portrait, and the signature-style name 「御凪くらげ」 at the bottom right. Use small handwritten Japanese captions beside each outfit, but keep them decorative and legible only in key places.

Visual style: Bright soft studio lighting, pastel airbrushed glow, semi-realistic fashion photography mixed with scrapbook illustration, rounded panels, translucent stickers, handwritten marker typography, sparkly gel-pen doodles, cute jellyfish motifs, hearts, stars, and small blue/pink annotation boxes.

Constraints: Show exactly 7 daily outfit cards plus 1 large portrait. Do not add extra days or extra main characters. No watermark, no app UI, no hard shadows, no dark background. Keep all clothing clearly visible and make the design feel like a Japanese Heisei retro outfit diary page.
